title: Logs Never Rotate: Growing Forever
description:
## The Situation

A service has been writing to `/var/log/app/service.log` for weeks. The file is now **2.4GB** and growing. Log rotation was configured but it's silently doing nothing.

The on-call engineer before you "set up logrotate" — but the logs are clearly not rotating. Something in the configuration is wrong.

## Your Task

1. Find why logrotate isn't rotating the logs
2. Fix the configuration
3. Force a rotation to confirm it works

## Useful Commands

```bash
# Check the logrotate config
cat /etc/logrotate.d/app

# Test the config (dry run)
logrotate -d /etc/logrotate.d/app

# Force rotation immediately
logrotate -f /etc/logrotate.d/app

# Verify rotation happened
ls -lh /var/log/app/
```
hints: Run logrotate -d /etc/logrotate.d/app to debug. It will tell you exactly what it would do and why it might be skipping rotation.
Logrotate skips files it can't find. Check the path in the config matches the actual log file location.

The config references /var/log/app/*.log but the file is named service.log — the path glob is correct. Look at the other settings: misuse/rotate/size can prevent rotation too. Check if the log file path in the config is exactly right.
